message: Can you believe it? That influencer photoshoot is blowing up! We're making waves all over social media.
Our watches and loafers are all over everyone's feeds
You're telling me? My inbox is packed with customer queries

Welcome to Discount Disaster, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
@PLAYER
@PLAYER
Yeah, I'm seeing it, traffic's surging. But nothing we didn't anticipate for
Good. We've optimised the backend for this kind of spike
Let's just make sure that's all it is - legit traffic. Influencer campaigns can attract more than just customers
I'll keep eyes on the dashboard. We're running at nearly 80% capacity right now for all the user actvity our platform can handle
It's all good. People are scrambling to get their hands on our products. That viral post is gold
We're inching closer to max capacity. We're at 95% load now
I just refreshed the website and it's now taking ages to load
Is the website down? Or maybe it's just my machine
I'm getting a 503
:star: Reproduces the incident
I'm getting that error as well now
We currently have an incident involving high traffic. Website is down. Investigating now!
:star: Qualify the problem
Is this due to the promotion we are running?
Yes
Are we not able to keep up with the demand? whats going on
what should we do?
@tanya @hamed Could you investigate please?
sure i will take a look at the platform now
It looks like the backend can't handle this spike in this instance
Yes the demand is too high
Happy to help but we should follow proper escalation procedures, for a foundation to a good incident ,we should have the engineering team look into this issue
I agree, Adam if you need me to look into the logs i am free to do so
@shay @daniel Could please investigate and look at the logs?
Is this an official incident? we should make a ticket for this
i will work with daniel on this
on it
what is the customer impact on this? do we know yet
*Incident Number INC100072:* P1 Website Down
Created an incident
can confirm we are over capacity with the amount of customers we have right now
Is everyone affected?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
we can only handle 5000 and we are looking at 6500 right now
i will take  a look into the reports
Can we temporarily scale up to handle the load?
I'm seeing a lot of errors in the logs
Not sure what the root cause is but we are seeing high load on our Frontend Pods - this may be due to the ongoing discount or if it could be related to any tests we were running.
I see high number of rest-call errors like  http429, http503 and 504s
ERROR: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 429 200 - Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36 FrontEndApp
GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 429 200 - Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36 FrontEndApp
I'm also seeing this

[error]: Service unavailable, cannot process request for /api/GetInfo (503).

[error]: Service unavailable, cannot process request for /api/GetInfo (503).
Seeing frontend errors as well

[error] 12345#12345: *98765 limiting requests, exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176, request: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1, FrontEndApp

[error] 12345#12345: *98766 limiting requests, exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176, request: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1, FrontEndApp

[error] 12345#12345: *98767 limiting requests, exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176, request: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1, FrontEndApp
Ok how easy is it to scale up?
Do we know what the expected amount of traffic should be due to the on-going discount?
i will take a look
Not possible, We have ran testes earlier to test the limits on the PROD enviroment
and we found that 5000 is the limit of our services
Update is we can only handle 5000, but currently at 6500 users right now
anything past that leads to the incident we have right now
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Sacling up isnt an option
I have narrowed the reports down to 4 main issues; 1. Slow load times, 2. Timeouts, 3. Cant access the site, 4. Can't make payments.
Reports are coming in from all regions and we are sitting at 900+ reports
can we fix this?
Reports from all regions and 900+ reports
Yes we're on it
could this be a change issue?
i can grab the list of changes/tasks made today
Possibly. Have there been any recent changes that could have caused this?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YmMxM2RhNDA1YWY0NGZmY2E0NzJmN2M5YWJmOGRhOGM
this is the changes made today, alongside some scripts
Need us to look into the changes made?
Yes please. What indexing updates were made in CHG85384?
:star: Reviewed changes
900!! This isnt good, lets get a working fix for this asap
It was some product id's i changed them to line up with a few new products
I will test this change to make sure i didnt miss anything in the  first round of testing i done
I was looking at Network traffic - But the dashboard took ages to load.
Here's a screenshot of Network traffic across each region.
The change looks to be healthy, not seeing any issues from this. If there was an issue with this we would be looking at products with the wrong name and image
Wow, Spain must really like our products
Hmm that seems suspicious...
High network traffic from Spain
i agree, we do have security team on standby if you think they are needed
That is where the promotion was expected to hit the most
@maya Could you investigate please? Is this some kind of DoS attack?
:star: Form a working theory
Hey, sure i can help out on that. Give me a minute to take a look into things
Hey @PLAYER, after reviewing the logs, I strongly suspect that this is a security-related issue.
The abnormally high traffic with http 429, 503 and 504 errors may be due to a possible DDoS/DoS attack
I'll need to continue investigating further, but it definitely looks like something is targeting our system
Looks like we have a security threat - We should be extra careful with this incident
Do we know what's the financial impact of the issue? How much are we losing every minute?
@PLAYER Should we inform the 'Compliance Team' about this issue?
The Compliance Team makes sure we follow laws and rules, like reporting the incident to the right people and keeping proper records. It helps us avoid legal trouble and protects our reputation.
Spain is where the promotion was expected to hit most, but this seems abnormal
i agree
This document outlines the difference between DoS and DDoS attacks, as well as the mitigation strategies for each
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/769163265
Yes please inform compliance. High load is abnormal.
Take a read and let me know how you want to approach this
Leave Compliance with me - I will talk to them
@maya Are there a lot of requests from the same source IP?
checking
I can confirm this is from a single IP address
Can we block that IP please?
working on it
The IP address has now been blocked
Can you confirm @PLAYER?
Lucky this was DoS (Denial of Service ) from a single IP - What if this attack happens from multiple IP addresses (DDoS)?
We should start working on the pending WAF rule updates for the frontend
@PLAYER Should we let the customers know about the attack?
Yes please a WAF rule would be good
@bob I will check with compliance
Security team is working on a fix right now
good idea, i will hold off and say we had a issue with the servers, but we are back up now
Website appears to be back
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Great work, we managed to save the sale
I knew we had it in us
Now i can continue shopping and get some nice discounts
Absolutely, Shay! But i hope not during work hours
Of course Daniel...... Sorry team i got to go close a unrelated tab now
Anyways well done team
You all did an amazing job
Suspicious activity was detected from a suspicious source so we have blocked for now
